data.title: HSBC downgrades Palo Alto Networks to Reduce due to high valuation
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (PANW) was downgraded to Reduce from Hold by HSBC due in part to its valuation.

Despite Palo Alto surpassing estimates in its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and increasing its full-year guidance, HSBC deemed the results as "sufficient" and "not transformational."

Palo Alto shares had dipped 2% during Friday morning market action.

The cybersecurity firm highlighted several key wins during its most recent quarter, including a $100M deal with a U.S. telecom company and a $33M deal with a U.S. federal agency. It also reached more than 7.5M seats for its secure browser, while bookings more than tripled year over year.

"However, with all of this considered, platformization and new projects have failed to reverse the overarching long-term trend of decelerating revenue growth, in our view," said HSBC analysts, led by Stephen Bersey, in an investor note. "Furthermore, we think the market is pricing in that growth will, in fact, accelerate; we see the reverse."

"Moreover, it is famously difficult to judge the success of product transitions in general, like platformization, by simply looking at platform wins, and growing revenue streams as legacy revenue can sometimes shift or be recategorized as new product revenue with a slight redefinition of what constitutes a 'new' sale," Bersey added.

HSBC retained its $157 target price on the stock.

data.title: AI Bubble or AI Boom? SA analysts share their thoughts
data.description:
Concerns across Wall Street are intensifying over whether the market is in an artificial intelligence ((AI)) driven bubble, as major equity benchmarks continue to face notable pressure in the month of November.

On Thursday, those worries sharpened after the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) swung dramatically, climbing more than 2% early in the session before reversing sharply and closing down more than 2%. The abrupt four-point intraday shift followed robust earnings from Nvidia (NVDA), a bellwether of the AI trade, and underscored the market’s increasingly fragile tone. 

The dramatic move has also further divided investors. Some warn the volatility signals a growing rush toward safety, while others argue the surge in AI spending reflects a durable, long-term infrastructure buildout rather than a speculative bubble. 

Below is what Seeking Alpha analysts are saying about the possibility of an emerging AI bubble: 

SA analyst Desmond Lachman stated: “Anyone doubting that we have an artificial intelligence-induced stock bubble has not been paying attention to the many warning signals that we are in bubble territory,” in When Will The Stock Market Bubble Burst?

SA analyst Rational Techne highlighted: “A clear-eyed survey of history reveals that things are about as obvious as they can be: we are currently in a bubble. Investors should de-risk their portfolio while they can because not everyone is going to be able to make it through the fire exits when the time comes,” in 8 Reasons We're Definitely In A Bubble. 

SA analyst Leo Nelissen said: “The current AI-driven market is not a bubble but rather a massive industrial buildout underpinned by real cash flows and cautious capital allocation,” in The AI Bubble Everyone Fears Doesn't Exist. 

SA analysts Jonathan Weber outlined: “Unlike the dot-com era, most AI leaders like NVDA, AMD, and ORCL have real business models and strong revenue growth, though some valuations are stretched… A broad market crash seems unlikely; diversified investors face manageable risk, but concentrated bets in unprofitable AI names carry higher downside potential,” in Should You Fear The AI Bubble? 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

Artificial IntelligenceETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ),

data.title: Oppenheimer thinks T-Mobile US to be hurt by looming industry price war; lowers rating
data.description:
• Oppenheimer on Friday downgraded T-Mobile US (TMUS) to "perform" from a previous investment rating of "outperform."
  • The research firm thinks T-Mobile will have a difficult time beating subscriber and FCF estimates after a decade of outsized share gains and margin expansion and believes overall industry subscriber growth is set to slow and will intensify competition.
  • "Industry price war will hurt flowshare and margins," Oppenheimer said in its note. "Competitive intensity is high but will increase with TMUS having the most to lose."
  • They noted that TMUS stock is trading at a steep premium to its peers, which would likely erode if the company's growth slows. 
  • TMUS currently does not have a price target from the research firm.

data.title: RTX JV wins $1.25B Iron Dome missile contract with Israel
data.description:
RTX (RTX) said Friday its Raytheon-Rafael Protection Systems joint venture was awarded a $1.25 billion contract to supply Israel with Tamir surface-to-air missiles, in a contract that includes missiles, missile kits and test equipment.

The companies said they used a $33 million capital investment to establish a new facility in Arkansas, which opened this month and will make missiles for the Iron Dome Weapon System and its U.S. variant SkyHunter.

Rafael developed Iron Dome, which has been operational since 2011 and has intercepted thousands of threats with a success rate exceeding 95%.

"This is the first production contract for the R2S joint venture and a major milestone for both Raytheon and Rafael," R2S CEO Jonathan Casey said. "The new Camden site is the first all-up-round production facility in the U.S. to manufacture Tamir and SkyHunter missiles."

Last month, RTX (RTX) reported better than expected Q3 adjusted earnings and revenues while raising its full-year profit and revenue forecast on rising demand for its missiles and aftermarket services.

data.title: Lilly market cap exceeds $1T in a first for healthcare
data.description:
• Eli Lilly (LLY) shares rose 1.7% shortly after the market opened on Friday to exceed the $1T market capitalization, becoming the first healthcare company to reach the milestone, fueled by investor interest in the company’s GLP-1 franchise.
  • Shares of the Indiana-based drugmaker hit an intraday high of $1,060.90 at about 9:50 AM ET,  following a report that the company and its rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) are set to sell their weight loss drugs directly to employers.

data.title: lululemon's Americas president to leave for Vail Resorts
data.description:
Lululemon athletica’s (LULU) president of the Americas and global guest innovation, Celeste Burgoyne, is leaving the company for a position as executive VP and chief revenue officer of Vail Resorts (MTN) after 19 years at the Canadian apparel manufacturer.

She will remain at the company until the end of December to ensure a smooth transition.

This is the second high-profile exit within the company in the past 18 months. The loss of both Burgoyne and Chief Product Officer Sun Choe “is not ideal” says Needham’s Tom Nikic, as both Burgoyne and Choe were viewed as the “key architects to lululemon’s ascendence,” singling out Burgoyne as a “key source of stability and consistency” at the company.

In conjunction with Ms. Burgoyne’s exit, lululemon (LULU) will consolidate regional leadership and has appointed André Maestrini as President and Chief Commercial Officer, effective immediately. Maestrini will report directly to lululemon (LULU) CEO Calvin McDonald and will oversee all of the company’s regions, stores, and digital channels.

Burgoyne’s exit is adding pressure to lululemon (LULU) shares, surrendering early premarket gains to trade unchanged at Friday’s open. Vail Resorts (MTN) is up a little over 1%. 

Source: Press Release

data.title: Wholesale inventories hold steady in August
data.description:
August wholesale inventories: 0.0% M/M to $907.9B vs. -0.2% consensus and +0.1% prior, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday.

Total inventories rose 1.1% from the revised August 2024 level.

Wholesale trade sales increased 0.1% vs. +1.3% prior (revised from +1.4%).

The August inventories/sales ratio, based on seasonally adjusted data, was 1.28, unchanged from July and down from 1.34 in August 2024, the Census Bureau said.

data.title: Intel CEO denies new hire taking trade secrets from TSMC: report
data.description:
Intel (INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan denied allegations of taking trade secrets from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) related to its recent hire of long-time TSMC executive Wei-Jen Lo, according to Bloomberg.

The 75-year-old Lo retired from TSMC in July after serving more than 20 years with the company. He was most recently the senior vice president of corporate strategy development for TSMC. He was instrumental in developing some of TSMC's advanced node technology. Intel recently hired Lo as vice president of research and development. 

TSMC has allegedly started an internal investigation to determine if Lo took trade secrets regarding advanced node technology, the report said. However, Tan steadfastly rejected the allegations.

"It's rumor and speculation," Tan said to Bloomberg during the Semiconductor Industry Association Awards in San Jose, Calif., on Thursday. "There's nothing to it. We respect IP."

Before joining TSMC in 2004, Lo worked at Intel for several years, serving as director of technology development and a plant manager. He earned his Ph.D. in solid-state physics and surface chemistry from the University of California at Berkeley.

Intel is attempting to strengthen its Foundry business, while TSMC continues to gain steam with its own, manufacturing chips for the world's biggest hardware companies, such as AMD (AMD), Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA).

Intel shares have surged 67% in 2025 after securing a significant slate of investments and deals. Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF) completed a $2B investment in Intel in late September. This followed Intel and Nvidia signing a deal that would see the duo co-develop PC and data center chips. Nvidia also said it would take a $5B stake in Intel. In August, the Trump Administration took an $11.1B, or 10%, stake in Intel.

Reports have also surfaced of Intel in talks with companies such as AMD and Apple to manufacture chips in its Foundry. Earlier this week, it was reported Qualcomm (QCOM) was possibly evaluating Intel Foundry for its data center ASIC business.

data.title: Wall Street edges higher as stocks rebound from the prior day’s volatile downturn
data.description:
Wall Street traded higher on Friday, a day after the Nasdaq Composite saw a dramatic session due to a tech-led sell-off.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last up by 0.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) had climbed up by 0.4% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) advanced by 0.3%. 

From a sector-by-sector point of view, eight of the 11 S&P segments are tracking higher in the green and are led by Communication Services. At the other end of the spectrum, Energy has been the worst performer on the session so far with oil (CL1:COM) lower by 2.2%. 

Treasury yields continue to tick lower as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down by 2 basis points to 4.07% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down by 2 basis points to 3.51%. 

Market participants are also still digesting a turbulent session on Thursday that sent the Nasdaq Composite on a dramatic round trip—from up more than 2% in the morning to down over 2% at the close.

“While we expect volatility in 2026, we do expect a positive return with the S&P 500 reaching 7500 by the end of next year,” Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth said. 

“As we enter 2026, the equity market has had great performance since the lows of the bear market in 2022. We expect 2026 to be the reset year for the leadership of the market in the big Growth Technology stocks. We believe this reset will position stocks to continue their leadership into the end of the decade. Despite this anticipated correction in the large cap area of the market, we don’t see leadership emerging from small caps,” Mary Ann Bartels added. 

On the economic front, there are a few Federal Reserve speakers lined up, including Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan and Boston Fed President Susan Collins. 

The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose slightly to 54.8 in November from 54.6 in the prior month. At the same time, Services PMI came in at 55 and manufacturing PMI came in at 51.9.

data.title: U.S. PMI Composite rises slightly more than expected in November
data.description:
The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose slightly to 54.8 in November from 54.6 in the prior month, above the 54.5 consensus, according to data released on Friday.

Services PMI: 55 vs. 54.6 expected and 54.8 in October.

Manufacturing PMI: 51.9 vs. 52 expected and 52.5 in October.

More on the U.S. Economy

  • Why the September jobs report will lead to more tariff cuts
  • NY Fed's Williams sees room for further rate adjustments in the near term
  • Morgan Stanley no longer expects a December Fed rate cut
